rulings.Case Title: FONGKOK ZHOU ET AL VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues Against Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. As a preliminary matter the Court notes that the moving party does not specify which causes of action, affirmative defenses, claims for damages or issue of duty are to be disposed of by the 5 issues identified in the Notice of Motion and repeated in the Separate Statement. The Court and opposing party are left to guess. For example, issue number 5 appears to be potentially directed at the causes of action for negligent and/or intentional infliction of emotional distress but does not dispose of the entirety of those causes of action, so the issue is not proper for summary adjudication. Since the moving party failed to comply with CCP 437c(f)(1) in framing its issues, the motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. In order to prevail on a motion for summary judgment the moving party must establish that there are no triable issues of fact as to any cause of action asserted against them and that the moving party is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. The causes of action for Negligent and Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress incorporate by reference all of the preceding 171 paragraphs of the operative complaint and seek damages associated with the defendants' duty regarding ownership, management, maintenance and construction of the property. These paragraphs include allegations of habitability, that are not addressed in the points and authorities as to the causes of action for negligent and intentional infliction of emotional distress. (Moving papers 18:4- 20:17). The Court recognizes that incorporation by reference is a generic form of allegation, but the burden is on the moving party to explore the allegations and the basis for each cause of action in the complaint (e.g. discovery seeking all facts supporting each cause of action) before a Motion for Summary Judgment is brought. If that is not done, the Plaintiff, as happened in this case, always has a plausible position that each subsequent cause of action arises partially out of allegations not specifically made within a given cause of action. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEILANI MERRILL VS. CALVIN FRANCIS GALDAL-GIBBS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2025 line 8. PLAINTIFF LEILANI MERRILL'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO ESTABLISH ADMISSIONS FROM DEFENDANT, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), Requests for Production of Documents and Things (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order.
